name: hig-components-system
description: Apple Human Interface Guidelines for system experience components including widgets, live activities, notifications, complications, and more.
capabilities: Provides Apple HIG guidance for widgets, live activities, and notifications
Explains complication design for watchOS with family-specific layouts
Documents App Clip requirements including size limits and discovery methods
Covers home screen quick actions, top shelf, and watch face design
References official Apple Developer documentation with direct links
Recommends appropriate system surfaces based on use case requirements
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains only markdown documentation files with Apple Human Interface Guidelines content. All 1196 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES: URLs are documentation references to developer.apple.com, not network calls; 'C2 keywords' and 'weak crypto' patterns appear in API documentation text, not executable code; 'external_commands' are code examples in markdown, not shell execution. The skill has no executable code, no network capabilities, and poses no security risk.
valueStatement: Designers and developers struggle to create compliant system experiences across Apple platforms. This skill provides authoritative Human Interface Guidelines for widgets, live activities, notifications, complications, and more.
userTitle: Design system experiences with Apple HIG
limitations: Contains documentation only - does not execute code or build components
References may become outdated as Apple updates HIG - verify with latest docs
Does not provide platform-specific implementation code samples
Limited to system experience components - use other skills for UI controls

useCases.title: iOS App Widget Design
useCases.description: Design a home screen widget for a fitness app showing daily activity rings.

useCases.title: Live Activity Implementation
useCases.description: Plan a live activity for food delivery tracking from order to arrival.

useCases.title: Watch Complication Planning
useCases.description: Design watch face complications for a weather app with multiple sizes.

promptTemplates.title: Widget Design Basics
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the key principles for designing an effective iOS home screen widget? I need to display news headlines.

promptTemplates.title: Live Activity Suitability
promptTemplates.prompt: Should my meditation timer app use a live activity? What are the design requirements for timer-based live activities?

promptTemplates.title: Notification Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a notification strategy for a task management app. What types of notifications are appropriate and how should they be grouped?

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Platform System Experience
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm building a ride-sharing app. Recommend the optimal system experiences for iOS, watchOS, and explain how they should work together for the rider experience.

bestPractices: Design for glanceability - users should understand content in 1-2 seconds
Respect system and update budgets to avoid throttling or disabled updates
Always deep-link to relevant content, never to the app root screen
antiPatterns: Displaying ads or promotional content in widgets or live activities
Sending notifications for purely marketing purposes without user value
Requiring full app install to complete tasks started in an App Clip

faq.question: What is the maximum size for an App Clip?
faq.answer: App Clips must be 10MB or less for cellular download. Keep them as small as possible for faster launch times, especially in low-bandwidth situations.

faq.question: How often can widgets update?
faq.answer: Widget update frequency depends on system budget and widget activity level. Typical ranges are 15-30 minutes for active widgets. The system may throttle updates based on battery and usage patterns.

faq.question: Can live activities appear on the Dynamic Island?
faq.answer: Yes, live activities can appear on Dynamic Island for iPhone 14 Pro and later. Design for both Dynamic Island compact/expanded states and Lock Screen presentation.

faq.question: What are complication families?
faq.answer: Complication families are size categories for watch face complications: modular small/large, circular small/medium/large, graphic corner/rectangular, and utilitarian small/large. Each requires distinct layout designs.

faq.question: Do home screen quick actions require user permission?
faq.answer: No, home screen quick actions (3D Touch/Haptic Touch) are system-managed and appear automatically when users long-press your app icon. No permission is needed.

faq.question: What content works best for tvOS top shelf?
faq.answer: Top shelf should showcase featured content: new episodes, trending items, continue watching, or personalized recommendations. Use high-quality artwork and keep content fresh to encourage engagement.
